ucs.title: [LLM] APT41 (Wicked Panda) DLL side-loading via known signed lure binary from staging path
ucs.description: APT41/Winnti's signature defense-evasion is dropping a legitimate signed third-party binary (DTHelper.exe from Mediatek BT, oleview.exe, BdReinit.exe, sllauncher.exe, chrome_frame_helper.exe, log.exe, mcods.exe, etc.) into a writable staging directory and side-loading a malicious DLL (ShadowPad / PlugX / Winnti core / KEYPLUG / SPYDER) from the same folder. This UC catches that exact tradecraft: a known APT41 lure binary running from a non-vendor path, with a sibling unsigned DLL imported within seconds.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) AS firstTime max(_time) AS lastTime FROM datamodel=Endpoint.Processes WHERE Processes.process_name IN ("BdReinit.exe","oleview.exe","DTHelper.exe","sllauncher.exe","chrome_frame_helper.exe","HPCustParticUI.exe","log.exe","mcods.exe","vmnat.exe") AND (Processes.process_path LIKE "%\\ProgramData\\%" OR Processes.process_path LIKE "%\\Temp\\%" OR Processes.process_path LIKE "%\\AppData\\%" OR Processes.process_path LIKE "%\\Public\\%" OR Processes.process_path LIKE "%\\PerfLogs\\%" OR Processes.process_path LIKE "%\\Intel\\%") AND NOT match(Processes.user,"\$$") BY host Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.process Processes.process_hash Processes.parent_process_name Processes.dest | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner host process_path [ | tstats summariesonly=true count AS dll_drop_count, min(_time) AS dll_time FROM datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem WHERE Filesystem.file_name="*.dll" AND (Filesystem.file_path LIKE "%\\ProgramData\\%" OR Filesystem.file_path LIKE "%\\Temp\\%" OR Filesystem.file_path LIKE "%\\AppData\\%" OR Filesystem.file_path LIKE "%\\Public\\%" OR Filesystem.file_path LIKE "%\\PerfLogs\\%") BY host Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.file_hash | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | rename file_path AS process_path file_name AS sideloaded_dll file_hash AS sideloaded_dll_sha256 ] | where dll_time <= firstTime + 600 AND dll_time >= firstTime - 1800 | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(dll_time) | table firstTime, host, user, process_name, process_path, process_hash, parent_process_name, process, sideloaded_dll, sideloaded_dll_sha256
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let _apt41_loaders = dynamic(["bdreinit.exe","oleview.exe","dthelper.exe","sllauncher.exe","chrome_frame_helper.exe","hpcustparticui.exe","log.exe","mcods.exe","vmnat.exe"]);
let _staging_paths = dynamic([@"\ProgramData\", @"\Temp\", @"\AppData\", @"\Public\", @"\PerfLogs\", @"\Intel\"]);
let Loaders = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where tolower(FileName) in (_apt41_loaders)
    | where FolderPath has_any (_staging_paths)
    | where FolderPath !startswith @"C:\Program Files" and FolderPath !startswith @"C:\Program Files (x86)"
    | project LoaderTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              LoaderName=FileName, LoaderPath=FolderPath, LoaderSHA256=SHA256,
              LoaderCmd=ProcessCommandLine,
              ParentName=InitiatingProcessFileName, ParentCmd=InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
DeviceImageLoadEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where FileName endswith ".dll"
| where FolderPath !startswith @"C:\Windows\"
| where FolderPath has_any (_staging_paths)
| join kind=inner Loaders on DeviceId, $left.InitiatingProcessFileName == $right.LoaderName
| where Timestamp between (LoaderTime .. LoaderTime + 5m)
| where FolderPath =~ LoaderPath   // sibling DLL = side-load
| where SHA256 != LoaderSHA256
| project LoaderTime, ImageLoadTime=Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          LoaderName, LoaderPath, LoaderSHA256,
          SideloadedDll=FileName, SideloadedDllSHA256=SHA256,
          ParentName, ParentCmd, LoaderCmd
| order by LoaderTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let _apt41_loaders = dynamic(["BdReinit.exe","oleview.exe","DTHelper.exe","sllauncher.exe","chrome_frame_helper.exe","HPCustParticUI.exe","log.exe","mcods.exe","vmnat.exe"]);
let _staging_paths = dynamic([@"\ProgramData\", @"\Temp\", @"\AppData\", @"\Public\", @"\PerfLogs\", @"\Intel\"]);
let Loaders = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | extend LoaderShort = tostring(split(NewProcessName, @"\")[-1])
    | where LoaderShort in~ (_apt41_loaders)
    | where NewProcessName has_any (_staging_paths)
    | where NewProcessName !startswith @"C:\Program Files"
    | project LoaderTime=TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, NewProcessName,
              LoaderShort, CommandLine, ParentProcessName;
// Pivot to Sysmon image-load (EID 7) for the actual side-loaded DLL when available
let ImageLoads = WindowsEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where Provider == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon" and EventID == 7
    | extend Image = tostring(EventData.Image),
             ImageLoaded = tostring(EventData.ImageLoaded),
             Signed = tostring(EventData.Signed),
             Hashes = tostring(EventData.Hashes)
    | extend LoaderShort = tostring(split(Image, @"\")[-1])
    | where LoaderShort in~ (_apt41_loaders)
    | where ImageLoaded endswith ".dll"
    | where ImageLoaded has_any (_staging_paths);
Loaders
| join kind=inner ImageLoads on Computer, LoaderShort
| where TimeGenerated between (LoaderTime .. LoaderTime + 5m)
| project LoaderTime, ImageLoadTime=TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
          Loader=NewProcessName, LoaderCmd=CommandLine, ParentProcessName,
          SideloadedDll=ImageLoaded, Signed, Hashes
| order by LoaderTime desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: APT41 DLL Side-Loading via Known Lure Binary from Staging Path
id: 8b3a4d20-a141-4e5f-bf41-9b7c12a5e001
status: experimental
description: Detects execution of legitimate third-party signed binaries that APT41 (Wicked Panda / BARIUM / Winnti) repeatedly abuses for DLL side-loading of ShadowPad, PlugX, Winnti and KEYPLUG, when launched from non-vendor staging directories.
references:
  - https://www.mandiant.com/resources/blog/apt41-us-state-governments
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0096/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026-05-07
tags:
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.t1574.002
  - attack.t1036.005
  - attack.g0096
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_lure:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\BdReinit.exe'
      - '\oleview.exe'
      - '\DTHelper.exe'
      - '\sllauncher.exe'
      - '\chrome_frame_helper.exe'
      - '\HPCustParticUI.exe'
      - '\log.exe'
      - '\mcods.exe'
      - '\vmnat.exe'
  selection_path:
    Image|contains:
      - '\ProgramData\'
      - '\Temp\'
      - '\AppData\'
      - '\Public\'
      - '\PerfLogs\'
      - '\Intel\'
  filter_legit:
    Image|startswith:
      - 'C:\Program Files\'
      - 'C:\Program Files (x86)\'
  condition: selection_lure and selection_path and not filter_legit
falsepositives:
  - Rare: legitimate copies of these vendor utilities staged outside install directories by IT for portable use
level: high
ucs.rationale: APT41 has been pinned to side-loading via this exact short-list of carrier binaries across Mandiant, Kaspersky and ESET reporting (DTHelper.exe→ShadowPad, sllauncher.exe→PlugX, oleview.exe & BdReinit.exe→Winnti, log.exe→KEYPLUG). The combination of (a) one of these specific binary names, (b) running outside its normal vendor install path, and (c) loading a sibling DLL within seconds is essentially

ucs.title: [LLM] APT41 web shell on Exchange/IIS chained to scheduled-task persistence (China Chopper / Antsword to schtasks)
ucs.description: APT41/BARIUM consistently exploits public-facing IIS or Exchange (e.g. ProxyLogon, Citrix, Log4Shell) to drop China Chopper / Antsword / regeorg web shells, then immediately runs recon (whoami, net, systeminfo) and creates a scheduled task as persistence (often masquerading under Microsoft\Windows\* paths). This UC fires when w3wp.exe spawns recon/LOLBin children AND a schtasks /create executes on the same host within 30 minutes — the hands-on-keyboard footprint that distinguishes APT41 from a noisy commodity web-shell scan.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) AS shell_time max(_time) AS shell_last FROM datamodel=Endpoint.Processes WHERE Processes.parent_process_name IN ("w3wp.exe","httpd.exe","tomcat.exe","java.exe") AND Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","whoami.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","ipconfig.exe","nltest.exe","quser.exe","reg.exe","sc.exe","wmic.exe","schtasks.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe") AND NOT match(Processes.user,"\$$") BY host Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.process_path | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner host [ | tstats summariesonly=true count AS sch_count min(_time) AS sch_time values(Processes.process) AS sch_cmd values(Processes.parent_process_name) AS sch_parent FROM datamodel=Endpoint.Processes WHERE Processes.process_name="schtasks.exe" AND (Processes.process LIKE "%/create%" OR Processes.process LIKE "%/CREATE%" OR Processes.process LIKE "%-Create%") BY host ] | where sch_time >= shell_time AND sch_time <= shell_time + 1800 | eval delay_sec = sch_time - shell_time | convert ctime(shell_time) ctime(sch_time) | table shell_time, host, user, parent_process_name, process_name, process, sch_time, delay_sec, sch_parent, sch_cmd
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let _shell_tools = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","whoami.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","ipconfig.exe","nltest.exe","quser.exe","reg.exe","sc.exe","wmic.exe","schtasks.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","hostname.exe"]);
let _web_servers = dynamic(["w3wp.exe","httpd.exe","tomcat.exe","java.exe","nginx.exe"]);
let WebShellChildren = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (_web_servers)
    | where FileName in~ (_shell_tools)
    // Strip baseline Exchange/IIS health-probe noise
    | where not(InitiatingProcessCommandLine has_any ("MSExchangeHealthManager","MSExchangeOWAAppPool") and ProcessCommandLine has "DefaultAppPool")
    | project ShellTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              IISParent=InitiatingProcessFileName, IISCmd=InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              ChildProc=FileName, ChildCmd=ProcessCommandLine;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where FileName =~ "schtasks.exe"
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("/create","/Create","/CREATE")
| project SchTaskTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName,
          SchTaskCmd=ProcessCommandLine,
          SchInitiator=InitiatingProcessFileName, SchInitiatorCmd=InitiatingProcessCommandLine
| join kind=inner WebShellChildren on DeviceId
| where SchTaskTime between (ShellTime .. ShellTime + 30m)
| extend DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', SchTaskTime, ShellTime)
| extend LooksMasqueraded = SchTaskCmd has_any (@"\Microsoft\\Windows\\", "WindowsUpdate", "DefenderUpdate", "OneDriveSetup", "AdobeFlashPlayer")
| project ShellTime, DeviceName, AccountName, IISParent, IISCmd,
          ChildProc, ChildCmd, SchTaskTime, DelaySec, SchTaskCmd, SchInitiator,
          LooksMasqueraded
| order by ShellTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let _shell_tools = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","whoami.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","ipconfig.exe","nltest.exe","quser.exe","reg.exe","sc.exe","wmic.exe","schtasks.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe"]);
let _web_servers = dynamic(["w3wp.exe","httpd.exe","tomcat.exe","java.exe","nginx.exe"]);
let WebShellChildren = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | extend ParentShort = tostring(split(ParentProcessName, @"\")[-1]),
             ChildShort  = tostring(split(NewProcessName,    @"\")[-1])
    | where ParentShort in~ (_web_servers)
    | where ChildShort in~ (_shell_tools)
    | project ShellTime=TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
              ParentShort, ChildShort, NewProcessName, CommandLine, ParentProcessName;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where EventID == 4688
| extend ChildShort = tostring(split(NewProcessName, @"\")[-1])
| where ChildShort =~ "schtasks.exe"
| where CommandLine has_any ("/create","/Create","/CREATE")
| project SchTaskTime=TimeGenerated, Computer, SchTaskCmd=CommandLine,
          SchParent=ParentProcessName
| join kind=inner WebShellChildren on Computer
| where SchTaskTime between (ShellTime .. ShellTime + 30m)
| extend DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', SchTaskTime, ShellTime)
| extend LooksMasqueraded = SchTaskCmd has_any (@"\Microsoft\Windows\", "WindowsUpdate", "DefenderUpdate", "OneDriveSetup", "AdobeFlashPlayer")
| project ShellTime, Computer, Account, ParentShort, ChildShort, CommandLine,
          SchTaskTime, DelaySec, SchTaskCmd, SchParent, LooksMasqueraded
| order by ShellTime desc
ucs.rationale: APT41 is publicly attributed to multiple ProxyLogon (CVE-2021-26855) and follow-on Exchange/IIS web-shell campaigns (Mandiant: 'APT41 Targets U.S. State Governments', 2022; CISA AA22-138B). Their post-exploitation playbook is consistently: w3wp child process → recon (whoami, systeminfo, net group) → schtasks /create with a Microsoft-style task path for ShadowPad/HIGHNOON persistence. Joining a w3w
